text:
There was once a village full of rabbits. They lived happily, grazed together, and had big balls. One day a fox moved in. He was crafty and sneaky.

He'd sit still all day watching them. One afternoon he decided to pounce. Swift as a flash, he grabbed one. The others stampeded.

They chased him. Jumping here, hopping there. The fox ran. Eventually, he tripped. Down went a bunny.

No one minded. They had lots more ball-sized holes afterward. Life went on. That fox.

They were lively. The rabbits loved music. They had paw-liful voices.

One rabbit picked a flute. He blew gently. Birds came.

They danced. The fox even joined. He didn't think much.

Next day, he did homework.

They sang. They were happy.

That fox never hunted again. They made ball courts. They called them Owl Eyes. They played there. That was their secret.

They lived happily
